element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Head-to-head racing fundamentals; basic mat rhythm control (left side only)
Dash plus timed jump execution; white box markers signal jump timing

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Olympics mode (can use either mat side)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Running + sustained air time; single jump mechanic
Long jump variant with three sequential jump markers; highest precision requirement
Progressive difficulty; must beat all six opponents in both dash and hurdles sequentially
Penalty system; three false starts = disqualification. Encourages controlled timing

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully completing all jumps without fouls
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical: rapid buildup during event execution (seconds per race), relief on completion, then reset for next opponent or event. Tournament mode chains tension across six sequential matchups.

difficulty_curve.early_game: 100M dash introduces basic mat control and rhythm; straightforward racing against first opponents
difficulty_curve.mid_game: 110M hurdles adds timing complexity with visual jump cues; opponent difficulty escalates across tournament bracket
difficulty_curve.late_game: Long jump and triple jump emphasize precision and sustained control; final tournament opponents present peak challenge
notable_spikes: Transition from dash to hurdles (adds jump mechanic)
Tournament progression: mid-bracket opponents show noticeable AI improvement
Triple jump introduction: requires three precise jump executions in sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential 1v1 races against six computer opponents. Each opponent must be beaten in both 100M dash and 110M hurdles. Three false starts result in disqualification per event.
All four events completed consecutively by up to six alternating players. Scoring referenced against 1982 Guinness world records. No tournament failure conditions.
100M dash and 110M hurdles require left side of mat only. Long jump and triple jump permit either side. Top speed dependent on which row of buttons is pressed.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All events (controls vary by event type)

sources_used: Wikipedia - Stadium Events (Gameplay section, Release section)
The Cutting Room Floor - Family Fun Fitness: Stadium Events (regional differences context)
